title: Ordered CatBoost categorical sidecar with blocked OOF blending
summary: Current best code manually frequency-encodes many high-cardinality race/driver combinations.
rationale: Current best code manually frequency-encodes many high-cardinality race/driver combinations. CatBoost’s ordered boosting and ordered target statistics are designed to handle such categories with less leakage than ad hoc encodings, and its docs explicitly discourage external one-hot preprocessing for these features.
implementation_hint: Train a sidecar CatBoostClassifier on mostly raw numerics plus raw categorical columns and a few interaction strings, using ordered boosting/CTR handling instead of manual freq and median encodings. If blending, use strictly fold-disjoint OOF predictions and sigmoid calibration; avoid isotonic if preserving AUC ranking is the priority.
expected_effect: May recover signal from rare drivers/races/compound interactions that manual encodings smooth away, while also adding ensemble diversity with minimal extra feature work.
risk: If folds are not truly forward/group blocked, ordered CTRs can still overstate CV; isotonic calibration can also alter AUC via ties.
